symptoms.symptom_name: Intense chronic pruritus, worse at night, with excoriation to bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritus is the defining symptom of atopic dermatitis; nocturnal worsening is characteristic.
Severe nocturnal itch is also classic for scabies — distribution and burrows discriminate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical flexural distribution (antecubital and popliteal fossae) with groin and axillae spared

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Flexural predilection is the hallmark adult atopic dermatitis pattern.
Contact dermatitis usually follows the contact site, not a symmetrical endogenous flexural pattern.
Scabies favours finger webs, wrists and genitals — sparing of the groin argues against it.
Impaired barrier function produces the widespread dryness underlying atopic eczema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lichenification (thickened, leathery skin) on wrists and neck

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lichenification reflects chronic rubbing/scratching — evidence of long-standing atopic eczema rather than an acute new rash.

symptoms.symptom_name: Personal history of allergic rhinitis (atopic diathesis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Personal atopy (allergic rhinitis) is part of the atopic triad and strongly supports atopic dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Environmental trigger — recent cold/dry weather and dry indoor heating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low humidity dries the skin barrier and is a classic atopic dermatitis flare trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, vesicles, honey-coloured crusts or weeping

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No monomorphic vesicles/erosions, pain or systemic features — screens eczema herpeticum negative.
No honey crusts, pustules or weeping — no current bacterial superinfection.
Elevated total IgE is common in atopy but non-specific and unnecessary for diagnosing this clinical flare — should NOT be ordered acutely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 125/80, HR 88, RR 16, SpO2 100%, Temp 36.7°C) and clear chest on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals and afebrile status support an uncomplicated flare with no systemic infection — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers this from the patient's report of no fever, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe sleep deprivation and functional impairment from nocturnal itch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marks flare severity and the need to break the itch-scratch cycle; drives the sedating-antihistamine-for-sleep decision.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Nocturnal pruritus + symmetrical flexural distribution + generalised xerosis + personal atopy together make atopic dermatitis the confident clinical diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: No acute investigation required — clinical diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Atopic dermatitis is a clinical diagnosis; no blood test or swab is needed for an uncomplicated flare.
Non-specific and unnecessary for this acute flare; ordering it is an error of commission (over-investigation).
Only indicated IF secondary bacterial superinfection or eczema herpeticum is suspected — neither is present today. Reserve for the safety-net scenarios.
